articles.title: Meta enters AI image model race in bid to court advertisers and subscribers
articles.summary: Meta has released a new AI model for creating images, Muse Image. The model will be available for consumers to access for free via the Meta AI app and site, WhatsApp direct messages, and Instagram Stories. Users who hit their free limit can purchase a subscription or wait until the limit resets. Muse Image will be used to power advertiser-specific image-generation tools as part of Meta's AI-powered Advantage Plus service.

articles.title: Behind Xbox's Big Layoffs, a Streaming Strategy That Failed
articles.summary: Xbox announced on Monday that it would lay off 3,200 employees and let go of five game studios. The company is aiming to reset itself after overspending on studios, staff, and games that people didn't want to play. Xbox plans to take a more streamlined approach to games and studios going forward. It will focus more on franchises like Minecraft, which is considered one of the most successful video games in the world.

articles.title: SpaceX just launched the 1st-ever nuclear-powered commercial satellite
articles.summary: The BOHR (Betavoltaic Orbital High-Reliability) satellite is a commercially built nuclear-powered cubesat created by Florida-based company City Labs. It was designed to test the company's proprietary NanoTritium betavoltaic micropower source in space for the first time. The NanoTritium device harnesses beta particles emitted from the radioactive decay of tritium and converts them directly into electricity using a semiconductor. It is meant to provide continuous power to spacecraft without a reliance on solar energy.

articles.title: Tesla Cybercab Includes More Powerful FSD Hardware
articles.summary: Production units of the upcoming Cybercab are running a more powerful version of Tesla's Full Self-Driving computer compared to the Model 3 or Model Y. The biggest change is the increased onboard memory, which will allow the computer to run much larger self-driving models. The extra computing muscle enables the Cybercab to be equipped with an SAE Level 4 Autonomous Mode - the Cybercab is designed to operate entirely without human intervention. Tesla started testing Cybercabs without steering wheels or pedals on public roads in Austin last week.

articles.title: How tech workers are feeling in 2026: a workforce splitting in two
articles.summary: Tech workers are either amplified by AI or shaken by it. The divide is shaping their feeling about work more than any title, tenure, or company. Burnout is surging, and optimism is fading. While productivity is up, the quality is questionable. Many tech workers believe the tech industry is in a state of chaos.

articles.title: herdr (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: herdr is an agent multiplexer that lives in the terminal. It gives users a view of every agent at a glance. Agents keep running even when users are detached, and sessions survive restarts. herdr can be used by agents through a pure socket API. Plugins are available.

articles.title: The People Who Will Thrive in the AI Age
articles.summary: AI doesn't seem to be taking away jobs and leaving humans with nothing to do. Studies have found that AI adoption has resulted in work becoming more intense, not less. People are using the time they save by using AI to take on new tasks. The people who are going to make a difference are the ones who will seek improvement and actively wrestle with AI to develop their own mental capabilities to accomplish more.

articles.title: China May Restrict Access to Its Most Powerful AI Models
articles.summary: Chinese authorities have held talks with major Chinese AI companies about whether to restrict foreign access to their most advanced models. No decisions have been made yet, but officials are considering options like a bar on public release or a limit to domestic use only. China's AI companies have become popular partly because they are free and open source. Closing access would mean surrendering the lever that drove China's rise.

articles.title: All Cars Sold in the EU Now Require a Camera Aimed at Your Face. It's Still Not Clear Where That Data Goes
articles.summary: The camera can never be truly switched off and keeps monitoring the driver at all times to make sure they're paying attention to the road.

articles.title: Microsoft Replaces OpenAI, Anthropic With Own AI in Some Apps
articles.summary: Apps like Excel and Outlook are now using Microsoft's MAI models rather than models from OpenAI and Anthropic.

articles.title: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with US companies as OpenAI, Anthropic costs surge
articles.summary: Recent model releases from Chinese companies are seen by many as highly competitive compared to leading frontier systems from companies in the US.

articles.title: Will Someone Finally Blink in the AI Spending War?
articles.summary: Big tech renting out excess compute is an indicator that the industry has overbuilt.

articles.title: Samsung to Get Jump on Apple's First Foldable With New Phones in July
articles.summary: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 is expected to feature a shorter and wider design that resembles Apple's planned folding iPhone.

articles.title: How to use AI agents better than 99% of people
articles.summary: Agents need a memory system built for how they actually read, write, and coordinate.
